markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Define last-writer, merge, conflict, and repair behavior for simultaneous operator/model rich document edits. This MT belongs to CRDTAndConcurrencyCore. Make rich document, graph mutation, and agent proposal state conflict-aware and recoverable.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
CRDT requirements apply to human plus agent concurrent draft state, rich document block state, graph mutation proposals, and EventLedger promotion, even when realtime multi-user UI is deferred.
AI/agent actions must carry stable actor/site IDs, leases, idempotency keys, and recovery receipts.
scope.expected_behavior: ConcurrentEditorSaveSemantics leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: ConcurrentEditorSaveSemantics is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.implementation_evidence: wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 0bcde5fc; CRDTAndConcurrencyCore: 41 tests/6 binaries green on real PostgreSQL. Pending adversarial code review.

concerns: NO LOST UPDATE -- DB-CONSTRAINT-GUARANTEED, not interleaving luck. decide_concurrent_save (save_semantics.rs:70-89) is a pure deterministic state-vector comparison (Equal->FastForward, DominatedBy->StaleWrite, Dominates->AheadOfHead, Concurrent->ConcurrentFork): same vectors always yield the same verdict regardless of wall-clock order. The actual append guard is in push_yjs_update: the optimistic 'before == head' check (yjs_bridge.rs:537) is a TOCTOU fast-path, but the real backstop is the UNIQUE index idx_kernel_crdt_updates_seq on (workspace_id, document_id, crdt_document_id, update_seq) (migration 0020:28-29). Two writers that both read head_seq=N and both attempt seq=N+1 cannot both insert; the loser gets a unique violation surfaced as a typed SequenceSlotRace denial (yjs_bridge.rs:595-602). So two actors can NEVER both win the same slot.

NO SILENT OVERWRITE: every non-accepted save returns a typed Conflict carrying BOTH a durable denial receipt id AND a KNOWLEDGE_CRDT_CONFLICT_DETECTED event (save_semantics.rs:187-203, record_conflict_receipt), idempotent on (crdt_document_id, update_id). The spec MUST 'manual edits leave actor/state-vector/denial receipts' is satisfied for the denied path.

Draft-log only (not authority): saves land in kernel_crdt_updates (storage_authority column, WP-KERNEL-002 draft log), never in a ProjectKnowledgeIndex authority table. Correct per spec 2.3.13.11 (drafts MAY use CRDT).

MINOR: this MT does not call guard_lease_for_write -- draft saves are not lease-gated. Acceptable since drafts are not authority, but means a writer without any lease can append draft updates. Noted, not blocking.
evasion_probes: Concurrent same-slot save race: simulated two envelopes with state_vector_before == current head; both pass the optimistic check; both target seq=N+1; the second INSERT violates idx_kernel_crdt_updates_seq UNIQUE -> SequenceSlotRace (not a silent overwrite, not a lost update). Verified the index is UNIQUE in 0020.
StaleBase with equal vectors: impossible by construction (push would have accepted); code defensively errors 'stale-base denial with fast-forward decision' (save_semantics.rs:182-184).
update_id reuse with different bytes: UpdateIdContentMismatch denial (yjs_bridge.rs:524-531). Idempotent identical resubmit: AlreadyStored.
Tried to make both ConcurrentFork writers commit: the second still collides on the unique seq slot -> one denied. No double-win path found.
evidence_checked: src/backend/handshake_core/migrations/0150_knowledge_crdt_denial_receipts.sql (durable denial home, FK to ledger, unique idempotency_key)

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
